methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 24 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
WB Group is a pre-IPO stage Defense & Security company founded by Piotr Wojciechowski, Adam Bartosiewicz, and Krzysztof Wysocki. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 10 firms across the Defense & Security sector: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers including Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, Shield AI, and Chaos Industries.

Peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable to this analysis. Of 24 same-sector peers identified, zero companies met the narrow filtering criteria of matching stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier. This insufficient clustering (minimum three datapoints required) means an all-sector median approach would introduce material distortion and is therefore excluded.

Without viable triangulation through comparable multiples or precedent transactions in the immediate peer set, this valuation framework cannot produce a defensible range or synthesis at present. The sparse population of pre-IPO, same-tier Defense & Security companies—despite a robust overall sector comp universe—represents the binding constraint. Forward valuation work on WB Group should either (1) establish additional transaction or financing data from private peers within the specified filters, or (2) accept broader stage or sector assumptions with explicit acknowledgment of the resulting inference risk. Total capital raised remains undisclosed, further limiting cross-validation approaches.
